---
title: "wetland_corr_plots"
author: "Michael Krochta"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)

# Paths 
data_dir_global <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data"
data_dir_local  <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/data/local_mod_data"
fig_dir         <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models/figures"
```

```{r}
# Helper: infer response label from filename ("Max WT", "Mean WT", "Range WT")
infer_response_from_name <- function(x) {
  x_low <- tolower(x)
  if (str_detect(x_low, "max"))   return("Max WT")
  if (str_detect(x_low, "mean"))  return("Mean WT")
  if (str_detect(x_low, "range")) return("Range WT")
  return("Response")
}

# Helper: infer site from filename (first token before '_' for locals; 'Global' for globals)
infer_site_from_name <- function(x, is_global = FALSE) {
  if (is_global) return("Global")
  nm <- basename(x)
  site <- str_split_fixed(nm, "_", 2)[,1]
  str_to_title(site)
}

# Helper: find the response column inside a dataframe robustly
# Tries common patterns; falls back to best regex match.
detect_response_col <- function(df, response_label) {
  # common candidates (edit if yours differ)
  candidates_by_label <- list(
    "Max WT"   = c("Max_WT","max_WT","MaxWT","maxwt","Max","max"),
    "Mean WT"  = c("Mean_WT","mean_WT","MeanWT","meanwt","Mean","mean"),
    "Range WT" = c("Range_WT","range_WT","RangeWT","rangewt","Range","range")
  )
  cand <- candidates_by_label[[response_label]]
  if (is.null(cand)) cand <- character(0)

  # exact or case-insensitive matches first
  found <- intersect(cand, names(df))
  if (length(found) > 0) return(found[1])

  # regex fallback by label keyword
  key <- tolower(str_replace(response_label, " WT", ""))
  rx  <- paste0("(?i)\\b", key, "\\b|^", key, "_|_", key, "$")
  m   <- names(df)[str_detect(names(df), rx)]
  if (length(m) > 0) return(m[1])

  # absolute last-ditch: first numeric column
  num_cols <- names(df)[map_lgl(df, is.numeric)]
  if (length(num_cols) == 0) stop("No numeric columns found to use as response.")
  message("Could not confidently detect the response column; using: ", num_cols[1])
  num_cols[1]
}
```

```{r}
# Compute Spearman correlations (per file)
corr_from_file <- function(path, is_global = FALSE) {
  df <- suppressMessages(readr::read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE))
  resp_label <- infer_response_from_name(path)
  site_label <- infer_site_from_name(path, is_global)

  resp_col <- detect_response_col(df, resp_label)

  # drop obvious non-predictor columns
  drop_like <- c("date","day","time","datetime","site","id","station","objectid")
  keep_num  <- df %>%
    select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
    select(-any_of(resp_col))

  # also drop numeric columns with zero variance
  keep_num <- keep_num %>% select(where(~ sd(.x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0))

  if (ncol(keep_num) == 0) {
    warning("No usable predictor columns found in: ", basename(path))
    return(tibble())
  }

  # compute Spearman rho + p for each predictor
  res <- map_dfr(names(keep_num), function(p) {
    x <- df[[resp_col]]
    y <- keep_num[[p]]
    ok <- is.finite(x) & is.finite(y)
    if (sum(ok) < 5) return(tibble(predictor = p, rho = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = sum(ok)))
    ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x[ok], y[ok], method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
    tibble(predictor = p, rho = unname(ct$estimate), p = ct$p.value, n = sum(ok))
  })

  res %>%
    mutate(site = site_label,
           response = resp_label) %>%
    select(site, response, predictor, rho, p, n)
}
```


```{r}
# Gather files
global_files <- c(
  file.path(data_dir_global, "max_global_data.csv"),
  file.path(data_dir_global, "mean_global_data.csv"),
  file.path(data_dir_global, "range_global_data.csv")
) %>% keep(file.exists)

local_files  <- list.files(data_dir_local, pattern = "_WT_model_data\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# Run correlations 
corr_global <- map_dfr(global_files, ~ corr_from_file(.x, is_global = TRUE))
corr_local  <- map_dfr(local_files,  ~ corr_from_file(.x, is_global = FALSE))
corr_all    <- bind_rows(corr_global, corr_local) %>% filter(!is.na(rho))

# Fixed prefix order for plotting
prefix_order <- c("WL","GWL","GWT","Water_area","Solar","Air","ppt","Elev","Size_km2")

get_prefix <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    str_starts(x, "GWL")        ~ "GWL",        # check longer first
    str_starts(x, "GWT")        ~ "GWT",
    str_starts(x, "WL")         ~ "WL",
    str_starts(x, "Water_area") ~ "Water_area",
    str_starts(x, "Solar")      ~ "Solar",
    str_starts(x, "Air")        ~ "Air",
    str_starts(x, "ppt")        ~ "ppt",
    str_starts(x, "Elev")       ~ "Elev",
    str_starts(x, "Size_km2")   ~ "Size_km2",
    TRUE                        ~ "zzz"         # anything else to the bottom
  )
}

# Build global predictor level order once (group first, then name)
pred_levels <- corr_all %>%
  mutate(.prefix = get_prefix(predictor),
         .pidx   = match(.prefix, prefix_order)) %>%
  arrange(.pidx, predictor) %>%
  distinct(predictor) %>%
  pull(predictor)


# Ensure consistent response column order
corr_all <- corr_all %>%
  mutate(response = factor(response, levels = c("Mean WT","Max WT","Range WT")),
         site     = factor(site))  # site order can be adjusted if you want
```

```{r}
# Plot: heatmaps per site (rows = predictors, columns = responses) ----
# Order predictors within each site by decreasing |rho| averaged across responses
# Ensure site order: Global first
site_levels <- c("Global", sort(setdiff(unique(corr_all$site), "Global")))

plot_df <- corr_all %>%
  filter(p < 0.05) %>%
  mutate(
    site      = factor(site, levels = site_levels),
    predictor = factor(predictor, levels = pred_levels)  # fixed, cross-site
  )


p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = response, y = predictor, fill = rho)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", rho)), size = 3) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    drop   = TRUE,
    limits = function(x) intersect(rev(pred_levels), x)
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166ac",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#b2182b",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    oob = scales::squish,
    name = "Spearman ρ"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ site, ncol = 4, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",              # legend underneath
    legend.key.width = unit(2, "cm"),        # stretch keys
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_colorbar(title.position = "top",
                               title.hjust = 0.5,
                               barwidth = 20, barheight = 0.6))  # horizontal bar

# Save
out_path <- file.path(fig_dir, "spearman_corr_heatmaps_daily.png")
ggsave(out_path, p, width = 14, height = 12, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
message("Saved: ", normalizePath(out_path, winslash = "/"))
print(file.info(out_path)$mtime)
```

*Corrplots for datasets to determine colinearity*
```{r}
# ----- Packages -----
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggcorrplot)

# ----- Paths -----
base_dir        <- "C:/Users/mbkro/OneDrive/Documents/wetland-ml-models"
data_dir_global <- file.path(base_dir, "data")
data_dir_local  <- file.path(base_dir, "data", "local_mod_data")
fig_dir         <- file.path(base_dir, "figures", "corr_plots")

dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ----- Settings -----
COR_METHOD <- "pearson"   # or "spearman"
DAILY_FUN  <- mean        # use mean for daily aggregation (switch to max if you prefer)

# ----- Helpers -----

# Try to extract a Date column from common date/time fields
detect_date_column <- function(df) {
  nms <- names(df)

  # 1) Already a Date?
  if ("Date" %in% nms && inherits(df$Date, "Date")) return(df$Date)

  # 2) String/number 'Date' that can be parsed
  if ("Date" %in% nms && !inherits(df$Date, "Date")) {
    d_try <- suppressWarnings(as.Date(df$Date))
    if (any(!is.na(d_try))) return(d_try)
    # try common formats
    d_try2 <- suppressWarnings(parse_date_time(df$Date, orders = c("Y-m-d","m/d/Y","d/m/Y","Ymd")))
    if (any(!is.na(d_try2))) return(as.Date(d_try2))
  }

  # 3) Datetime variants
  dt_names <- c("Datetime","DateTime","date_time","dateTime","timestamp","Timestamp")
  dt_col   <- dt_names[dt_names %in% nms]
  if (length(dt_col) > 0) {
    dt <- df[[dt_col[1]]]
    if (inherits(dt, "POSIXct") || inherits(dt, "POSIXt")) return(as.Date(dt))
    dt_try <- suppressWarnings(parse_date_time(dt, orders = c(
      "Y-m-d H:M:S","Y-m-d H:M","m/d/Y H:M:S","m/d/Y H:M","Ymd HMS","Ymd HM"
    )))
    if (any(!is.na(dt_try))) return(as.Date(dt_try))
  }

  # 4) Separate Date + Time columns → use Date
  if (all(c("Date","Time") %in% nms)) {
    d_try <- suppressWarnings(parse_date_time(paste(df$Date, df$Time),
                                              orders = c("Y-m-d H:M:S","m/d/Y H:M:S","m/d/Y H:M","Ymd HMS","Ymd HM")))
    if (any(!is.na(d_try))) return(as.Date(d_try))
  }

  # 5) Looks like nothing usable
  return(NULL)
}

# Keep only predictor columns (numeric, no responses, no date/id-like)
select_predictors <- function(df) {
  # Numeric-ify softly
  df_num <- df %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.))))

  # Drop obvious non-predictors by name
  drop_name_pat <- "(?i)^(date|datetime|time|timestamp|id|objectid)$"
  df_num <- df_num %>% select(!matches(drop_name_pat))

  # Keep only numeric columns
  df_num <- df_num %>% select(where(is.numeric))

  # Drop obvious response columns (WT / temperature variants)
  resp_pat <- "(?i)(^|_)((mean|max|range).*(wt|temp|temperature)|(mmwt|mean_wt|max_wt|range_wt|mean_temp|max_temp|range_temp))($|_)"
  df_num <- df_num %>% select(!matches(resp_pat))

  # Drop columns with <3 finite values or zero variance
  keep_cols <- df_num %>%
    summarise(across(everything(), ~{
      v <- .x[is.finite(.x)]
      (length(v) >= 3) && sd(v) > 0
    })) %>%
    as.list() %>% unlist()

  df_num[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]
}

# Aggregate predictors to daily using DAILY_FUN (default = mean)
aggregate_predictors_daily <- function(df) {
  dcol <- detect_date_column(df)
  preds <- select_predictors(df)

  # If no date detected, just return predictors as-is (hourly or whatever)
  if (is.null(dcol)) {
    message("  No date/datetime found → using raw rows (no daily aggregation).")
    return(preds)
  }

  dat <- bind_cols(.date = dcol, preds)

  daily <- dat %>%
    filter(!is.na(.date)) %>%
    group_by(.date) %>%
    summarise(across(-.date, ~ DAILY_FUN(., na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")

  # After aggregation, drop zero-variance / too-sparse again
  keep_cols <- daily %>%
    select(-.date) %>%
    summarise(across(everything(), ~{
      v <- .x[is.finite(.x)]
      (length(v) >= 3) && sd(v) > 0
    })) %>% as.list() %>% unlist()

  daily %>%
    select(-.date) %>%
    { .[, keep_cols, drop = FALSE] }
}

# Build correlation matrix safely
safe_cor <- function(df_pred, method = COR_METHOD) {
  if (ncol(df_pred) < 2) return(NULL)
  suppressWarnings(cor(df_pred, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = method))
}

# Save heatmap for one file
process_one_file <- function(filepath) {
  nm <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(filepath))
  message("Processing: ", nm)

  df <- readr::read_csv(filepath, show_col_types = FALSE)

  # ---- daily aggregation of predictors ----
  preds_daily <- aggregate_predictors_daily(df)

  if (ncol(preds_daily) < 2) {
    message("  Skipped (fewer than 2 usable daily predictor columns).")
    return(invisible(NULL))
    }

  cmat <- safe_cor(preds_daily)
  if (is.null(cmat)) {
    message("  Skipped (no correlation matrix).")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  # Plot heatmap (upper triangle) with white background
  p <- ggcorrplot(
    cmat,
    hc.order = TRUE,
    type = "upper",
    lab = FALSE,
    outline.col = "grey85",
    ggtheme = ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Predictor Correlations (", COR_METHOD, ", daily ", deparse(substitute(DAILY_FUN)), "): ", nm)
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      panel.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      plot.title        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, margin = margin(b = 8))
    )

  out_png <- file.path(fig_dir, paste0(nm, "_corr_heatmap_daily.png"))
  ggsave(out_png, p, width = 9.5, height = 7.5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  message("  Plot saved: ", out_png)

  invisible(out_png)
}

# ----- Files to process -----
global_files <- c(
  file.path(data_dir_global, "mean_global_data.csv"),
  file.path(data_dir_global, "max_global_data.csv")
) %>% keep(file.exists)

site_files <- list.files(
  data_dir_local,
  pattern = "\\.csv$",
  full.names = TRUE
) %>% keep(file.exists)

all_files <- c(global_files, site_files)

if (length(all_files) == 0L) {
  stop("No CSV files found. Check the paths.")
}

# ----- Run -----
walk(all_files, process_one_file)

message(
  "\nDone. Heatmaps → ", fig_dir,
  "\nAggregation: daily ", deparse(substitute(DAILY_FUN)), " on predictor columns.",
  "\n(If no date field was found in a file, it falls back to raw rows.)"
)



```
